lines.english: Lesson 31
lines.chinese: 第31课
lines.raw: Lesson 31 | 第31课

lines.english: A lovable eccentric
lines.chinese: 可爱的怪人
lines.raw: A lovable eccentric | 可爱的怪人

lines.english: Why did the shop assistant refuse to serve Dickie?
lines.chinese: 为什么店员拒绝为迪基服务？
lines.raw: Why did the shop assistant refuse to serve Dickie? | 为什么店员拒绝为迪基服务？

lines.english: True eccentrics never deliberately set out to draw attention to themselves.
lines.chinese: 真正的怪人从不刻意引人注目。
lines.raw: True eccentrics never deliberately set out to draw attention to themselves. | 真正的怪人从不刻意引人注目。

lines.english: They disregard social conventions without being conscious that they are doing anything extraordinary.
lines.chinese: 他们无视社会习俗，却没意识到自己在做任何非凡的事情。
lines.raw: They disregard social conventions without being conscious that they are doing anything extraordinary. | 他们无视社会习俗，却没意识到自己在做任何非凡的事情。

lines.english: This invariably wins them the love and respect of others, for they add colour to the dull routine of everyday life.
lines.chinese: 这总能让他们赢得他人的爱戴和尊重，因为他们为平淡的日常生活增添了色彩。
lines.raw: This invariably wins them the love and respect of others, for they add colour to the dull routine of everyday life. | 这总能让他们赢得他人的爱戴和尊重，因为他们为平淡的日常生活增添了色彩。

lines.english: Up to the time of his death, Richard Colson was one of the most notable figures in our town.
lines.chinese: 直到去世，理查德·科尔森都是我们镇上最著名的人物之一。
lines.raw: Up to the time of his death, Richard Colson was one of the most notable figures in our town. | 直到去世，理查德·科尔森都是我们镇上最著名的人物之一。

lines.english: He was a shrewd and wealthy businessman,
lines.chinese: 他是一位精明富有的商人，
lines.raw: He was a shrewd and wealthy businessman, | 他是一位精明富有的商人，

lines.english: but most people in the town hardly knew anything about this side of his life.
lines.chinese: 但镇上大多数人对他生活的这一面几乎一无所知。
lines.raw: but most people in the town hardly knew anything about this side of his life. | 但镇上大多数人对他生活的这一面几乎一无所知。

lines.english: He was known to us all as Dickie and his eccentricity had become legendary long before he died.
lines.chinese: 他被我们称为迪基，他的怪癖在他去世前很久就已成为传奇。
lines.raw: He was known to us all as Dickie and his eccentricity had become legendary long before he died. | 他被我们称为迪基，他的怪癖在他去世前很久就已成为传奇。

lines.english: Dickie disliked snobs intensely.
lines.chinese: 迪基非常讨厌势利小人。
lines.raw: Dickie disliked snobs intensely. | 迪基非常讨厌势利小人。

lines.english: Though he owned a large car, he hardly ever used it, preferring always to go on foot.
lines.chinese: 尽管他有一辆大车，但他几乎从不用它，总是喜欢步行。
lines.raw: Though he owned a large car, he hardly ever used it, preferring always to go on foot. | 尽管他有一辆大车，但他几乎从不用它，总是喜欢步行。

lines.english: Even when it was raining heavily, he refused to carry an umbrella.
lines.chinese: 即使下大雨，他也不撑伞。
lines.raw: Even when it was raining heavily, he refused to carry an umbrella. | 即使下大雨，他也不撑伞。

lines.english: One day, he walked into an expensive shop after having been caught in a particularly heavy shower.
lines.chinese: 一天，他在一场大雨中淋湿后走进一家昂贵的商店。
lines.raw: One day, he walked into an expensive shop after having been caught in a particularly heavy shower. | 一天，他在一场大雨中淋湿后走进一家昂贵的商店。

lines.english: He wanted to buy a ￡300 watch for his wife,
lines.chinese: 他想为妻子买一块300英镑的手表，
lines.raw: He wanted to buy a ￡300 watch for his wife, | 他想为妻子买一块300英镑的手表，

lines.english: but he was in such a bedraggled condition that an assistant refused to serve him.
lines.chinese: 但他衣衫褴褛，店员拒绝为他服务。
lines.raw: but he was in such a bedraggled condition that an assistant refused to serve him. | 但他衣衫褴褛，店员拒绝为他服务。

lines.english: Dickie left the shop without a word and returned carrying a large cloth bag.
lines.chinese: 迪基一言不发地离开了商店，然后提着一个大布袋回来了。
lines.raw: Dickie left the shop without a word and returned carrying a large cloth bag. | 迪基一言不发地离开了商店，然后提着一个大布袋回来了。

lines.english: As it was extremely heavy, he dumped it on the counter.
lines.chinese: 因为袋子很重，他把它扔在柜台上。
lines.raw: As it was extremely heavy, he dumped it on the counter. | 因为袋子很重，他把它扔在柜台上。

lines.english: The assistant asked him to leave, but Dickie paid no attention to him and requested to see the manager.
lines.chinese: 店员让他离开，但迪基没有理会他，要求见经理。
lines.raw: The assistant asked him to leave, but Dickie paid no attention to him and requested to see the manager. | 店员让他离开，但迪基没有理会他，要求见经理。

lines.english: Recognizing who the customer was,
lines.chinese: 经理认出了这位顾客，
lines.raw: Recognizing who the customer was, | 经理认出了这位顾客，

lines.english: the manager was most apologetic and reprimanded the assistant severely.
lines.chinese: 非常抱歉，并严厉训斥了店员。
lines.raw: the manager was most apologetic and reprimanded the assistant severely. | 非常抱歉，并严厉训斥了店员。

lines.english: When Dickie was given the watch, he presented the assistant with the cloth bag.
lines.chinese: 当迪基拿到手表时，他把布袋递给了店员。
lines.raw: When Dickie was given the watch, he presented the assistant with the cloth bag. | 当迪基拿到手表时，他把布袋递给了店员。

lines.english: It contained ￡300 in pennies.
lines.chinese: 里面装着300英镑的便士。
lines.raw: It contained ￡300 in pennies. | 里面装着300英镑的便士。

lines.english: He insisted on the assistant's counting the money before he left--30, 000 pennies in all!
lines.chinese: 他坚持要店员在他离开前数清钱——总共3万枚便士！
lines.raw: He insisted on the assistant's counting the money before he left--30, 000 pennies in all! | 他坚持要店员在他离开前数清钱——总共3万枚便士！

lines.english: On another occasion, he invited a number of important critics to see his private collection of modern paintings.
lines.chinese: 另一次，他邀请了一些重要的评论家参观他的现代绘画私人收藏。
lines.raw: On another occasion, he invited a number of important critics to see his private collection of modern paintings. | 另一次，他邀请了一些重要的评论家参观他的现代绘画私人收藏。

lines.english: This exhibition received a great deal of attention in the press,
lines.chinese: 这次展览受到了媒体的广泛关注，
lines.raw: This exhibition received a great deal of attention in the press, | 这次展览受到了媒体的广泛关注，

lines.english: for though the pictures were supposed to be the work of famous artists,
lines.chinese: 因为尽管这些画被认为是著名艺术家的作品，
lines.raw: for though the pictures were supposed to be the work of famous artists, | 因为尽管这些画被认为是著名艺术家的作品，

lines.english: they had in fact been painted by Dickie.
lines.chinese: 但实际上它们是迪基画的。
lines.raw: they had in fact been painted by Dickie. | 但实际上它们是迪基画的。

lines.english: It took him four years to stage this elaborate joke simply to prove that critics do not always know what they are talking about.
lines.chinese: 他花了四年时间策划这个精心设计的玩笑，只是为了证明评论家并不总是知道他们在说什么。
lines.raw: It took him four years to stage this elaborate joke simply to prove that critics do not always know what they are talking about. | 他花了四年时间策划这个精心设计的玩笑，只是为了证明评论家并不总是知道他们在说什么。
